programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Disenchanted investigative journalist Martin Scarsden is told by his boss to write a follow-up piece a year after a mass shooting of five parishioners by a charismatic young priest in the small rural town of Riversend.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin Scarsden decides to stay on in Riversend to dig deeper into the mystery of Byron Swift, a well-liked priest who suddenly murdered five of his parishioners.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: At teenager Allen's wake, Martin learns that the boy was not as innocent as first portrayed, with Robbie telling him he was persuaded by Byron to drop drug charges against him.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin believes he has found the link between the shooting victims and asks for Robbie's help in accessing the case files; a further clue implicates one of the victims.

programme.title: Black Snow
programme.desc: When a time capsule is opened and a secret is unearthed, Detective James Cormack is put on the trail of a murderer from a 1994 case that was never solved.

programme.title: Black Snow
programme.desc: After the discovery of Isabel's hair, those involved with the time capsule project in 1994 are at the forefront of Cormack's investigation.

programme.title: Black Snow
programme.desc: Rumors spread around Ashford, and suspicion lands on Joe, Isabel's father; as Cormack interviews the time capsule kids, more secrets emerge.

programme.title: Black Snow
programme.desc: Cormack investigates the link between Ezekiel's missing cousins. "The lost boys," and Isabel's murder as a new person comes forward with evidence.

programme.title: Black Snow
programme.desc: Turner concludes that Billy's death was an accident, but Cormack suspects foul play and believes it's linked to the missing workers and Isabel's murder.

programme.title: Black Snow
programme.desc: Cormack tries to track Anton down to question him, but he's disappeared, and when Cormack and Hazel discover that Kalana is also missing, they fear the worst.

programme.title: The Audacity
programme.desc: Duncan, almost tech titan, must prove he and his company are F-able; JoAnne provides therapy with benefits; Orson lands from Baltimore; the party for Dr. Green ends in a cat burglary.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Disenchanted investigative journalist Martin Scarsden is told by his boss to write a follow-up piece a year after a mass shooting of five parishioners by a charismatic young priest in the small rural town of Riversend.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin Scarsden decides to stay on in Riversend to dig deeper into the mystery of Byron Swift, a well-liked priest who suddenly murdered five of his parishioners.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: At teenager Allen's wake, Martin learns that the boy was not as innocent as first portrayed, with Robbie telling him he was persuaded by Byron to drop drug charges against him.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin believes he has found the link between the shooting victims and asks for Robbie's help in accessing the case files; a further clue implicates one of the victims.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: In 2003 Zoe Jacobs disappeared during her 21st birthday party; many believe Zoe ran away, but others suspect she was murdered; when police officer Sam Kahlil discovers Zoe's backpack, Detective James Cormack is called in to investigate.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: New evidence gives Nadja and Sam hope that Zoe is still alive, but as Cormack puts together a timeline of Zoe's disappearance, the old theory that Zoe drove to Brisbane doesn't add up.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: Cormack invites Sam to examine the contents of Zoe's backpack and probe her memory for clues; re-examining CCTV footage from the night she disappeared, he discovers Zoe actually drove to Xanadu, the rural property where Joseph was living.

programme.title: The Audacity
programme.desc: Duncan, almost tech titan, must prove he and his company are F-able; JoAnne provides therapy with benefits; Orson lands from Baltimore; the party for Dr. Green ends in a cat burglary.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: While the company revels in the glory of its successful Jaguar pitch, Lane reaches breaking point; Sally is reunited with an old friend.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don suffers with a crippling toothache; the partners consider expanding; Pete meets a stranger on a train.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don spearheads a new campaign; Roger gets unsettling news; Betty takes in a houseguest. Peggy gets an important call.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don spearheads a new campaign; Roger gets unsettling news; Betty takes in a houseguest; Peggy gets an important call.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don has a disagreement with a client; Pete and Trudy entertain some guests; Peggy tries to motivate the staff.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: The partners try to keep a secret campaign under wraps; An old friend visits Joan; Megan prepares for a love scene.
